key_insight: PINN-based training makes turbulence closures solver-agnostic and geometry-generalizable, enabling fast screening of closures that transfer across bluff-body wakes.
engineering_relevance: This gives CFD engineers a practical path to learn stable RANS closures that can be deployed in standard finite-element solvers and trained even from PIV data when DNS is infeasible.
method: Impose the RANS residual directly in a PINN via automatic differentiation, removing dependence on a specific CFD discretization.
Train four closure families across six 2D bluff-body wakes: tensor-basis stress models, a non-local kinetic-energy-transport model, a learned-length-scale variant, and a direct force model.
Improve deployment stability with input-gradient smoothing and a Lipschitz constraint before freezing the learned closure in a conventional solver.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on six wake cases at Re=10^4 and tested with a strict leave-one-shape-out protocol; the direct force closure achieved the best out-of-sample mean-velocity and drag performance, with LOSO drag error around 8.5%.
watch_item: The learned closure must still remain stable when embedded in a downstream solver, so solver coupling and extrapolation outside the training geometry set remain important risks.
paper_title: Generalizable turbulence closures across bluff-body shapes by PINN-based solver-agnostic training
